mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and datasources within Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer infrastructure as code or GitOps workflows. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes CRs and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Grafana Operator Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a Grafana Instance
steps.description:
This step involves creating a custom resource (CR) for Grafana to deploy an instance with specific configurations.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> This command creates a Grafana instance with basic configurations.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana Instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana

```

> This command lists all Grafana instances and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss for any Grafana instances managed by the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana --all

```

> This command uninstalls the Grafana Operator from the cluster.
> This command deletes all Grafana instances managed by the operator.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana configurations. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana

```

> Back up the current Grafana instance configurations.
> Upgrade the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the Grafana instance is still running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not have been created or may have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Check the Grafana CR status with 'kubectl get grafana'. If not found, recreate it.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Grafana'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the Grafana pod status with 'kubectl get pods' and logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC settings may not be correctly configured for the Grafana Operator.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the necessary RBAC roles and bindings are set up for the operator.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Invalid configuration'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The provided configuration for the Grafana instance may have syntax errors.

**Fix:**
Review the Grafana CR YAML for any syntax issues and correct them.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD handling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance listed with 'kubectl get grafana' and its status should be 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
